title: Leidos to Develop Autonomous Uncrewed Aerial Resupply System for U.S. Marine Corps
text: Leidos (NYSE:LDOS), a FORTUNE® 500 science and technology leader, was recently awarded a new prime contract to develop an uncrewed aircraft system (UAS) that can autonomously resupply forward-deployed ground forces. The firm-fixed-price, multiple-award contract has a period of performance of 18 months to build a single prototype for the Marine Corps.

title: Leidos CEO Roger Krone Looks Back On A Stellar Career, And Reflects On What He Has Learned
text: Roger Krone's career is emblematic of the defense industry's transformation since the Cold War ended.

title: 3 Dirt-Cheap Stocks That Could Skyrocket
text: Tyson Foods may be problem-prone, but it reliably bounces back from its recurring challenges. The travel industry is on the mend, even if that rebound isn't currently reflected in Expedia's stock price.

title: Why Leidos Believes It Is A Good Cultural Fit For The JADC2 Challenge
text: A different kind of defense contractor makes its bid for the biggest networking project in military history.
